hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2 (res 193/195) and TM5 (208/209) by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will improve selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective for SSTR2 binding but insufficient for achieving selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to disrupt off-target interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved-pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions and improve selectivity.
Introduce mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to break conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
